title: My Terrible Day
learningGoal: The learner will read a short diary entry to identify instances of the causative-passive form and determine who was forced to do what by whom.
instructions: Read this short diary entry written by a very tired salaryman. Pay attention to the particle 'ni', which indicates the person who forced him to do things.

contentItems.text: Remember: The causative-passive (saserareru / asareru) is the ultimate form for complaining about what you had to do.

readingText:
きのうは さいあくな ひ でした。
わたしは ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。
よる、ともだちに さけを のまされました。
そして、きょう、ははに やさいを たべさせられました。
とても つかれました。
textInTargetLanguage:
きのうは さいあくな ひ でした。
わたしは ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。
よる、ともだちに さけを のまされました。
そして、きょう、ははに やさいを たべさせられました。
とても つかれました。
baseLanguageSupportText: Yesterday was the worst day. I was forced to do overtime by the boss. At night, I was forced to drink alcohol by a friend. And today, I was forced to eat vegetables by my mother. I am very tired.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: わたしは ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Watashi wa buchou ni zangyou wo saseraremashita.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: I was forced to do overtime by the boss.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: よる、ともだちに さけを のまされました。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Yoru, tomodachi ni sake wo nomasaremashita.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: At night, I was forced to drink alcohol by a friend.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: ははに やさいを たべさせられました。
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: I was forced to eat vegetables by my mother.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Who forced the writer to do overtime?
What did the friend force the writer to do?
What did the mother force the writer to do?
Write the romaji for the word meaning 'overtime work'.

expectedSummaryPoints: The boss forced him to do overtime.
A friend forced him to drink.
His mother forced him to eat vegetables.

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent reading! You successfully navigated a series of complex causative-passive sentences.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the use of the particle 'ni'. In causative-passive sentences, 'ni' marks the person who is doing the forcing.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Notice how the 'I was forced' subject ('Watashi wa') is only stated once and then implied for the rest of the text.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: きのうは さいあくな ひ でした。わたしは ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。よる、ともだちに さけを のまされました。そして、きょう、ははに やさいを たべさせられました。とても つかれました。

hintText: Look closely at 'nomasaremashita'. It is the short causative-passive form of 'nomu' (to drink).
commonMistakes: Thinking the writer forced their friend to drink (reading it as causative instead of causative-passive).
